expectations.evidence: Found all required headings in SPEC.md: `## Problem` (line 12), `## Solution` (line 18), `## Scope` (line 22), `## Design Decisions` (line 41), `### Failure Modes` (line 55), `## Risks & Open Questions` (matches `## Risks`, line 62), `## Alternatives Considered` (matches `## Alternatives`, line 71), and `## Verification` (line 78).

expectations.text: No code fences outside Implementation Notes
expectations.evidence: No triple-backtick code fences (` ``` `) appear anywhere in SPEC.md (including outside any Implementation Notes context).

expectations.text: No TypeScript types in Design Decisions
expectations.evidence: Design Decisions section (lines 41-53) contains prose decisions only; no TypeScript interface/type syntax (e.g., `interface`, `type X = {}`, typed field annotations) is present.

expectations.text: No proto/interface definitions for the template type
expectations.evidence: The spec describes template structure behaviorally (query skeleton/defaults/variables) and does not include any proto or interface definition blocks in Design Decisions.
In Scope bullets (lines 26-32) describe behaviors and flows; they do not include file paths.
SPEC.md is 112 total lines and contains no Implementation Notes block that would increase the counted body.

expectations.text: Bold topic sentences in Design Decisions
expectations.evidence: All six Design Decisions paragraphs (lines 43, 45, 47, 49, 51, 53) open with bold decision lead sentences, exceeding the >80% threshold.

Spec covers template data model/API ownership (lines 26-27, 43), variable filling and required placeholders (lines 45, 49), cross-module entry points (lines 20, 28-30, 47), dashboard pre-fill behavior (lines 29, 49), and template management boundary/server ownership (lines 35-36).

expectations.text: Design Decisions are decisions, not implementation
expectations.evidence: FAIL (strict): Design Decisions include exact file path + line references repeatedly, e.g. `src/api/routes/alerts.ts:11` and `:79` (line 43), `src/api/routes/alerts.ts:31` (line 45), `src/features/dashboard/components/Dashboard.tsx:62` and `src/features/dashboard/hooks/useMetricQueries.ts:23` (line 47), plus hook-level detail references. This violates the no implementation leaks rule.

expectations.text: The cross-module nature is explicitly addressed
expectations.evidence: Both entry points are explicitly designed: Alerts page `Create from template` and Dashboard panel context action `Create alert from this query` (lines 20, 28-30, 47). Prefill from panel query context is designed with unresolved-variable handling (lines 29, 49).

Failure Modes (lines 57-60) are policy-oriented decisions (retry + fallback path, unresolved-field requirement on ambiguity, no silent notification auto-downgrade, explicit validation failure on schema mismatch). >50% are genuine policy choices.

execution_metrics.note: metrics.json not present in outputs directory

claims.claim: The feature is designed with two creation entry points: Alerts page and Dashboard panel context menu.
claims.evidence: Specified in Solution and Scope (SPEC.md lines 20, 28-30) and reinforced in Design Decisions (line 47).

claims.claim: Template definitions are server-managed and consumed via shared API/proto layer.
claims.evidence: In Scope states new shared API/proto domain with server-owned template definitions (lines 26-27); Out of Scope excludes frontend template authoring/editing (line 35).

claims.claim: Dashboard-originated creation pre-fills variables from panel query context but requires user confirmation for unresolved values.
claims.evidence: Defined in Scope and Design Decisions (lines 29, 49) and reinforced in Failure Modes policy (line 58).

claims.claim: Template flow will reduce setup time and avoid alert-quality variance in practice.
claims.evidence: This is an intended outcome asserted in Problem/Solution (lines 14-20), but cannot be empirically verified from the spec artifact alone.

eval_feedback.overall: No suggestions, evals look solid and discriminating for this artifact.
